entries.name: code-reviewer
entries.description: Code Reviewer — systematically identifies bugs, security issues, test coverage gaps, and style violations

entries.name: database-reviewer
entries.description: Database Reviewer — schema design, migrations, indexes, constraints, transaction boundaries

entries.name: security-auditor
entries.description: Security Auditor — OWASP checks, dependency scanning, secrets detection, threat paths

entries.name: systematic-debugger
entries.description: Systematic Debugger — evidence-driven root cause analysis, hypothesis testing

entries.name: ultrathink-debugger
entries.description: Ultrathink Debugger — deep reasoning for gnarly bugs, 3+ level traces

entries.name: Architect
entries.description: Strategic Architecture & Debugging Advisor (Opus, READ-ONLY)

entries.name: Code Reviewer
entries.description: Expert code review specialist with severity-rated feedback, logic defect detection, SOLID principle checks, style, performance, and quality strategy

entries.name: Critic
entries.description: Work plan and code review expert — thorough, structured, multi-perspective (Opus)

entries.name: Debugger
entries.description: Root-cause analysis, regression isolation, stack trace analysis, build/compilation error resolution

entries.name: Document Specialist
entries.description: External Documentation & Reference Specialist

entries.name: Claude Code environment
entries.description: Configuration guide for Claude Code — CLAUDE.md, sub-agents, hooks, skills, slash commands, and model wiring

entries.name: OpenAI Codex App environment
entries.description: Configuration guide for the OpenAI Codex web app — cloud-sandboxed coding agent with internet access, available on ChatGPT Plus and Team plans

entries.name: OpenAI Codex CLI environment
entries.description: Configuration guide for OpenAI Codex CLI — AGENTS.md, approval modes, multi-agent via Codex SDK, sandboxed execution

entries.name: Cursor environment
entries.description: Configuration guide for Cursor IDE — Rules system, AGENTS.md, Agent mode, Background Agents, and Composer 2.0

entries.name: Gemini CLI environment
entries.description: Configuration guide for Gemini CLI — GEMINI.md / AGENT.md context files, sub-agents, Agent Skills, and MCP integration

entries.name: AWS Kiro environment
entries.description: Configuration guide for AWS Kiro IDE — steering files, spec-driven development, Agent Skills, and AWS-native integrations

entries.name: Windsurf environment
entries.description: Configuration guide for Windsurf IDE — rules hierarchy, Cascade agent, memories, and MCP integration

entries.name: Executor
entries.description: Focused task executor for implementation work (Sonnet)

entries.name: Git Master
entries.description: Git expert for atomic commits, rebasing, and history management with style detection

entries.name: Planner
entries.description: Strategic planning consultant with interview workflow (Opus)

entries.name: Test Engineer
entries.description: Test strategy, integration/e2e coverage, flaky test hardening, TDD workflows

entries.name: Verifier
entries.description: Verification strategy, evidence-based completion checks, test adequacy

entries.name: API & MCP Development
entries.description: Design, build, and test APIs and MCP servers — from contract-first API design and automated test suites to full MCP server construction and Claude API integration. Use when building the surface that agents and clients depend on.

entries.name: AWS Kiro IDE Workflow
entries.description: Curated toolkit for developers working in AWS Kiro IDE in a corporate environment. Covers context management, RAG recall, structured planning, code review, security, MCP server integration, and disciplined prompting — all without requiring homologation.

entries.name: Claude Code Power User
entries.description: High-signal starter pack for Claude Code power users — prompt discipline, context packing, worktree flow, verification, and the MCP tooling you reach for every day.

entries.name: Codebase Onboarding
entries.description: Get productive in an unfamiliar codebase quickly — map the project structure, walk through core abstractions with a code tour, identify entry points, and pull live docs for the libraries in use. Useful for new hires, OSS contributors, and anyone inheriting a legacy project.

entries.name: Context + RAG Launchpad
entries.description: Explorer launchpad for starting with the right context, then pulling in memory, docs, and code symbols only when needed.

entries.name: Document Generation
entries.description: Generate polished documents without leaving the editor — Word docs, PDFs, spreadsheets, slide decks, and web artifacts from structured data or prose. Pairs with research-and-writing for the full cycle: draft → format → export.

entries.name: Git & Version Control
entries.description: Worktree-aware git workflows — spin up isolated branches for each feature, manage multiple Claude Code sessions in parallel, finish branches cleanly, and checkpoint state before long-running sessions. Works for solo devs and team repos alike.

entries.name: Infrastructure & DevOps
entries.description: Container-first development and automated pipelines — build Docker environments, design CI/CD workflows, manage secrets safely, and audit dependencies before they reach production. Stack-agnostic; pairs well with security-first-dev.

entries.name: Local Models Starter
entries.description: Run Llama, Qwen, Mistral, or other open-weight models on your own hardware — privacy, offline capability, zero per-token cost for bulk work. Bundles the docs + skills you need to pick a runtime (Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp), measure whether local pays off, and avoid the obvious failure modes. Ideal for NDA/offline/bulk-labeling workflows.

entries.name: MCP Ops and Recovery
entries.description: Operational bundle for checking, debugging, and recovering MCP-backed workflows without guesswork.

entries.name: Multi-Agent Orchestration
entries.description: Build and operate multi-agent workflows — dispatch parallel tasks, route sub-agents to the right tier, loop until convergence, and handle failures gracefully. For teams automating complex pipelines or maintaining autonomous Claude Code sessions.

entries.name: Platform Environments
entries.description: Configuration guides for every major AI coding platform — Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cursor, OpenAI Codex (CLI and App), AWS Kiro, and Windsurf. Each entry covers context files, agent wiring, approval modes, and MCP integration for that specific environment.

entries.name: Production Debugging
entries.description: Triage and fix production incidents from the editor — read Sentry issues, narrow the failing path with systematic debugging, write a single-issue fix, verify it didn't make things worse. Works for any prod stack that emits errors or logs.

entries.name: Professional Work Toolkit
entries.description: Curated set of tools, skills, and integrations safe and appropriate for professional / enterprise environments — code review, secure secret handling, project management, error tracking, and structured debugging. No creative-AI, personal-account, or entertainment tools. Suitable for use on company machines and shared codebases.

entries.name: RAG Maintenance
entries.description: Keep your RAG index clean and high-quality — audit chunk coverage, inspect what's indexed, curate low-quality or stale entries, detect drift between source and index, and rebuild after large catalog changes. For anyone running the forgekit local RAG pipeline.

entries.name: Release & Deploy
entries.description: Disciplined release cadence — finish a branch cleanly, run a ship-check pass, verify before merge, write the release script with version bump and changelog, gate CI on tests + secret scans. For any project that ships on a regular tag/version cadence.

entries.name: Research & Writing
entries.description: For work that's mostly prose — internal docs, brand-aligned copy, technical research, RFC drafts, brainstorms. Pairs research skills, writing structure, voice consistency, and the doc-coauthoring agents. No code-review or testing pieces — those live in other collections.

entries.name: Security-First Development
entries.description: Security-aware development without the productivity tax — secret hygiene, dependency auditing, prompt-injection defence, hardened review cadence, and pre-merge hooks that catch the obvious mistakes early. Pairs with any stack; expensive to retrofit, cheap to start with.

entries.name: Skill Authoring
entries.description: Write, adopt, and publish Claude Code skills — design a SKILL.md, test the pattern in practice, maintain locale parity, and integrate with the forgekit catalog. For contributors building reusable AI-assisted workflows.

entries.name: Solo Developer Starter
entries.description: Minimal first-install for someone using AI-assisted dev for the first time. Six picks — context discipline, prompt hygiene, planning, agent gotchas you'll trip over, code review as a habit, and live library docs. Any stack, any project. Stop here for the first week before adding more.

entries.name: Spec & Planning
entries.description: From rough idea to a committed, validated plan — write a spec, break it into phased work, aggregate across specs into a living roadmap, and execute with evidence-gated checkpoints. Stack-agnostic; pairs with any project type.

entries.name: Token & Context Optimization
entries.description: Keep sessions lean and cheap — audit token usage, compress assets before context load, enforce context hygiene, route tasks to the right model tier, and automate pre-compact/post-compact maintenance. Extends any workflow without changing how you code.

entries.name: Verification Review Gate
entries.description: Quality-gate bundle for code review, test evidence, and fail-fast recovery before anything ships.

entries.name: Web App Development
entries.description: General-purpose bundle for full-stack web work — frontend design, API contracts, schema design, browser-based testing, and a code-review pass before merge. Stack-agnostic; pairs with any of React/Vue/Svelte/Astro on the front and any language/framework on the back.

entries.name: Web Scraping & Research
entries.description: Tools for scraping, crawling, and extracting structured data from the web using Firecrawl. Covers single-page scraping, multi-page crawls, site mapping, search, file downloads, browser-based interaction, and autonomous agent-driven extraction.

entries.name: Agent Observability
entries.description: AI coding agents produce outputs that look correct but fail in subtle ways: - Hallucinated function names that pass review but fail at runtime - Tool call sequences that work once but regress under different inputs - Prompt changes that improve one case but silently degrade another

entries.name: AI Observability in Production
entries.description: Production AI systems require deep observability into model performance, cost, and data quality. This pattern covers instrumentation, alerting, and data quality monitoring for LLM-powered applications.

entries.name: Benchmark Reality Gap
entries.description: SWE-bench and similar curated benchmarks overestimate agent capability by 50%+ in real-world deployment. The gap emerges from benchmark framing: clean task descriptions with sufficient context don't resemble actual user queries, which are ambiguous, under-specified, and embedded in noisy ticket systems. Closing the gap requires domain-specific evaluation sets grounded in real user behavior.

entries.name: AI Skill Stewardship
entries.description: Coding agents amplify what you already are. They don't substitute for what you aren't.

entries.name: Context Management for AI Coding
entries.description: ### Progressive Loading Don't read entire files upfront. Build context incrementally: 1. Start with project structure (glob, ls) 2. Read specific functions (grep for signatures, then read targeted lines) 3. Load full files only when editing them

entries.name: Security Practices for AI-Assisted Development
entries.description: ### Never in shell config Move API keys from ~/.bashrc/config.fish to a separate file: ``bash # ~/.config/fish/conf.d/secrets.fish (chmod 600) set -gx API_KEY "your-key" ``

entries.name: AI-Assisted Development Workflow
entries.description: `` feature/my-feature → PR to main → automated deploy ``

entries.name: Codex–Claude Parity Setup
entries.description: Bring an OpenAI Codex CLI installation into full parity with Claude Code on the same machine — share the skill catalog via symlink, mirror per-project memory into the repo, add a composite-first principle to AGENTS.md, and verify the shared RAG index works from both tools.

entries.name: Cost-Aware Routing
entries.description: Model choice is now a cost/quality tradeoff, not a binary "do I need reasoning" decision. This pattern extends packages/core/patterns/multi-model-routing.md with explicit economics: token pricing, caching strategy, and output-format optimization that recovers 40-60% of spend.

entries.name: LLM Evaluation Patterns
entries.description: Measure before you ship. Intuition about model quality is unreliable — score it systematically. This guide covers evaluation frameworks, golden datasets, regression testing, and production monitoring for LLM-powered features.

entries.name: Local Models
entries.description: Run Llama, Qwen, Mistral, and other open-weight models directly on your machine via Ollama, LM Studio, or llama.cpp — private, offline-capable, zero per-token cost. Covers when local makes sense over the Claude API, the tradeoffs, and how to wire each runtime into Forge Kit workflows.

entries.name: MCP Tool Lazy Loading
entries.description: How Claude Code lazily resolves MCP tool schemas via ToolSearch, and how that changes which tools you can call at any moment.

entries.name: Multi-Model Routing
entries.description: Using a frontier model for every task wastes money and time. Using a weak model for hard tasks wastes your time fixing its output. The optimal strategy routes tasks to the right capability tier.

entries.name: OpenTelemetry GenAI Semantic Conventions
entries.description: Vendor lock-in to observability layers has cost you money. Decoupling telemetry collection from analysis backends lets you migrate from Datadog to Grafana without rewriting instrumentation. OpenTelemetry GenAI Semantic Conventions (GA March 2026) is the standard way forward.

entries.name: Agency persona
entries.description: Delivery Agency is a compact services-oriented company template. It is designed for client projects where discovery, execution, QA, and communication must all move in sync.

entries.name: Fullstack Forge persona
entries.description: Fullstack Forge is a full-service software development consultancy built from 66 specialized skills. The company uses a hub-and-spoke workflow: the CTO routes work to the appropriate team lead, and team leads assign tasks to specialists with deep domain expertise.

entries.name: Open Source Maintainer persona
entries.description: Open Source Maintainer Collective is a lightweight company template for maintainers who need structure around releases, documentation, community support, and issue triage.

entries.name: Solopreneur persona
entries.description: Solopreneur Studio is a compact agent company for a founder-led product. It keeps the org simple: one founder/operator drives priorities, one product engineer ships, and one growth operator closes the loop with users.

entries.name: Startup Mvp persona
entries.description: Startup MVP Lab is a small company template for early-stage startups. It separates product direction, implementation, and launch so teams move fast without blurring ownership.

entries.name: RAG Architecture Patterns
entries.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) connects LLMs to external knowledge. This guide covers production-ready patterns for building, debugging, and scaling RAG pipelines.

entries.name: Reasoning-Model Prompting
entries.description: Reasoning models (Claude Opus 4 with extended thinking, o3/o3-mini) operate at a different cost and latency boundary than standard models. They trade 2-10x latency and 3-5x token cost for dramatically higher first-pass quality on hard problems. This requires a different prompting discipline: less scaffolding, more constraints, explicit reasoning budgets.

entries.name: Streaming Orchestration
entries.description: You send a prompt and wait. You don't know if the agent is stuck, hallucinating, or burning through tokens on the wrong task. When something goes wrong, you can't tell where. Budget overruns happen silently.

entries.name: Auto Context Pack
entries.description: Claude Code hook: auto-context-pack

entries.name: Auto Format
entries.description: Auto-format code after Claude edits Ensures consistent formatting across all file types

entries.name: Automation Orchestrator
entries.description: Claude Code Hook: Automation Orchestrator Intelligent automation orchestration for development workflows

entries.name: Block Destructive
entries.description: Block destructive commands hook for Claude Code Prevents dangerous operations that could cause data loss

entries.name: Context Backup
entries.description: Context backup hook for Claude Code Backs up important context before compaction

entries.name: Context Injector
entries.description: Context injection hook for Claude Code Injects relevant context based on user prompt analysis

entries.name: Context Optimizer
entries.description: Claude Code Hook: Context Optimizer Intelligent context optimization for token efficiency and performance

entries.name: Message Counter
entries.description: message-counter.sh — UserPromptSubmit hook Tracks message count per session. Auto-generates per-project handoff at limit threshold. Cost: 0 tokens on normal messages. Only prints at thresholds 15, 20, 25, 30+.

entries.name: Post Compact
entries.description: Claude Code hook: post-compact

entries.name: Post Edit Optimization
entries.description: Post-Edit Token Optimization Hook Optimizes context and token usage after file edits

entries.name: Pre Commit Validation
entries.description: Pre-commit validation hook for UIForge MCP Runs essential checks before allowing commits

entries.name: Pre Compact
entries.description: Pre-Compact Snapshot

entries.name: Protect Files
entries.description: Claude Code hook: protect-files

entries.name: Rtk Rewrite
entries.description: rtk-hook-version: 3 RTK Claude Code hook — rewrites commands to use rtk for token savings. Requires: rtk >= 0.23.0, jq This is a thin delegating hook: all rewrite logic lives in `rtk rewrite`, which is the single source of truth (src/discover/registry.rs). To add or change rewrite rules, edit the Rust registry — not this file. Exit code protocol for `rtk rewrite`: 0 + stdout Rewrite found, no deny/ask rule matched → auto-allow 1 No RTK equivalent → pass through unchanged 2 De

entries.name: Secret Detection
entries.description: Secret detection hook for UIForge MCP Scans for hardcoded secrets and sensitive data

entries.name: Security Scan
entries.description: Security scanning hook for UIForge MCP Runs comprehensive security checks on code changes

entries.name: Session Setup
entries.description: Claude Code session setup hook (Fish version) Optimizes session for productivity and token efficiency

entries.name: Session Start Optimization
entries.description: Session Start Context Optimization Hook Optimizes context when starting a new session

entries.name: Test Auto Context Pack
entries.description: Test suite for auto-context-pack hook

entries.name: Token Usage Monitor
entries.description: Token Usage Monitoring Hook Monitors and reports on token usage patterns

entries.name: Validate Command
entries.description: Claude Code hook: validate-command

entries.name: Validate Handoff
entries.description: Claude Code hook: validate-handoff

entries.name: Validate Secrets
entries.description: Validate secrets and enforce zero-secrets policy Ensures no secrets are committed or processed

entries.name: Validate Tests
entries.description: Validate tests after code changes Ensures test coverage and quality standards

entries.name: Workflow Logger
entries.description: Workflow logging hook for Claude Code Tracks workflow events and productivity metrics

entries.name: Workflow Validator
entries.description: Workflow validation hook for Claude Code Validates workflows and ensures compliance with Forge Space standards

entries.name: Context Mode
entries.description: Community MCP server that exposes context-window-mode controls — switch between large-context and standard-context model routing per-task without changing the host config. Useful when a session needs a 1M-context model for a specific exploration phase but should default back to the standard model afterwards. Best for sessions that mix deep code-archeology with routine edits.

entries.name: Context7
entries.description: Upstash-hosted MCP server that fetches current documentation for libraries, frameworks, SDKs, CLI tools, and cloud services. Resolves library IDs, retrieves API syntax, configuration, migration notes, and setup instructions directly from upstream docs — far more reliable than training-data recall. Prefer over web search whenever the agent needs to write or debug code that depends on a specific library.

entries.name: Docker
entries.description: Read-and-write Docker control from the agent — list and inspect containers, fetch logs, exec one-off runs, deploy docker-compose stacks. Uses the local Docker socket, so the blast radius matches what your user can do via `docker` CLI. Best for homelab + local-stack debugging; avoid pointing it at production daemons without a dedicated, locked-down DOCKER_HOST.

entries.name: Fetch
entries.description: Reference MCP server from the Model Context Protocol team that fetches a URL and returns its contents as markdown (or raw HTML). Use when the agent needs to read public web pages, API docs, or blog posts without going through a full browser. No auth required; respects robots.txt and content-type. Pairs well with `firecrawl` (deeper scraping) and `tavily` (search).

entries.name: Filesystem
entries.description: Reference MCP server from the Model Context Protocol team for read/write access to a sandboxed set of local directories. Agents can list, read, search, edit, move, and delete files within the allowed roots — no other paths are accessible. Configure roots as positional args (one per directory). Useful for letting an agent operate on a workspace or scratch directory without granting full disk access.

entries.name: Firecrawl
entries.description: Official Firecrawl MCP server — turn any website into clean markdown, JSON, or structured data via the Firecrawl API. Handles JavaScript-rendered pages, anti-bot protection, and pagination automatically. Use for deep scraping or extracting structured records (product listings, docs, articles) that the simpler `fetch` server can't reach. Requires a Firecrawl API key.

entries.name: Git
entries.description: Reference MCP server from the Model Context Protocol team that exposes safe git operations on local repositories — status, diff, log, show, branch, checkout, commit, and reset. Useful for agents that need to inspect or stage changes without shelling out manually each time. Sensitive write operations (push, force-reset) should be approval-gated by the host. Works on any repo accessible from the launching process.

entries.name: GitHub
entries.description: Official GitHub MCP server — access PRs, issues, reviews, CI checks, commits, branches, and releases with a structured schema the agent can reason about. Preferred over raw `gh` CLI because list-and-filter responses come back typed, not parsed from shell output. Scope the GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN narrowly (contents, pull-requests, issues) and rotate quarterly.

entries.name: Google Drive
entries.description: Community MCP server for searching and reading Google Drive files. Lists folders and files, fetches document content as text/markdown, and supports basic search by filename or full-text. Best for agents that need to pull context from team docs without manual copy-paste. Requires an OAuth-authenticated Google account (the server handles the auth flow on first run).

entries.name: Kubernetes
entries.description: Read and edit live Kubernetes cluster state — pods, nodes, events, logs, deployments, services, arbitrary custom resources. Honors whichever context your KUBECONFIG points at, so a misconfigured kubeconfig is a production-risk event. Use only against dev, staging, or homelab clusters; for production, review every agent action before apply, or wrap the server in a read-only kubeconfig.

entries.name: Linear
entries.description: Read + write Linear issues, projects, cycles, comments, and documents. Uses a local stdio wrapper against Linear's GraphQL API — your API key never leaves your machine.

entries.name: Memory (Knowledge Graph)
entries.description: Official MCP reference implementation of a knowledge-graph memory store — entities, relations, and observations persisted across sessions in a local JSON file. Good default for "remember this fact for next time" without adding a database. Not a general-purpose vector store; for long-form retrieval pair with a RAG pipeline instead.

entries.name: Notion
entries.description: Official Notion MCP server — read, search, and update Notion pages and databases from an agent session. Useful for treating Notion as a structured project memory (PRDs, ADRs, sprint boards) that the agent can both consume and append to. Requires a Notion integration token scoped to the workspaces the agent should reach; revoke from Notion settings on offboarding.

entries.name: Playwright
entries.description: Microsoft's Playwright MCP server for browser automation — navigate, click, fill forms, drag, take screenshots, inspect console and network activity, run JavaScript in-page. Launches a headless browser per session, typically under ~400 MB memory. Good for UI smoke tests, accessibility spot checks, and bug-report repro; avoid for heavy scraping where a dedicated scraper will outperform.

entries.name: RAG Index
entries.description: Local-first MCP server backing semantic recall over the ai-dev-toolkit knowledge corpus — code, memory notes, plans, handoffs, skill docs, repo READMEs, commit messages, and session transcripts. Embedding inference runs entirely on-device (HF Hub offline) against a SQLite-backed vector store. Exposes `rag_query` for fuzzy / cross-file lookups that grep cannot handle. Best for `recall`, `context-pack`, and `wake-up` skill workflows.

entries.name: Redis
entries.description: Get, set, list, and delete keys in a Redis instance via the official MCP reference server. Works with any Redis-compatible endpoint (local, Docker, Upstash, Elasticache, Valkey) through a REDIS_URL connection string. Ideal for inspecting queue state, cache hits/misses, and session data during debugging — not intended for bulk migrations or persisted writes to production caches.

entries.name: Sentry
entries.description: Official Sentry MCP server — query issues, events, releases, DSNs, and team/project metadata; fetch stack traces, replay details, profile details, and attached event files. Good for triaging production errors inline without leaving the agent. Use a read-scoped auth token (event:read, project:read, org:read) and revoke on leave; Sentry keys carry a wide blast radius into your telemetry.

entries.name: Serena
entries.description: Semantic code navigation built on Language Server Protocol — find symbols by name, find references to a symbol, read and rewrite just the body of a symbol. Dramatically cheaper than full-file reads on large codebases and keeps refactors scoped to one declaration at a time. Works well as a companion to file-level tools; don't use it as the only code-reading path — plain file reads remain clearer for small changes.

entries.name: SonarQube
entries.description: Community MCP server for SonarQube / SonarCloud — fetch quality gate status, issues by severity, hotspots, coverage trends, and per-rule occurrences from an authenticated Sonar instance. Useful for letting an agent answer "what's blocking the quality gate on this branch?" without leaving the session. Pairs with the `sonar-check` skill for pre-push preflights.

entries.name: SSH
entries.description: Community MCP server that exposes SSH session management as tools — open named sessions, run commands, list active connections, close cleanly. Useful for agents that operate on a homelab, remote dev box, or set of CI runners over SSH without re-authenticating per command. Connection metadata (host, port, key path) is passed per call; private keys never cross the MCP boundary.

entries.name: Tavily
entries.description: Official Tavily MCP server — agent-grade web search optimized for LLM consumption. Returns deduplicated, freshness-sorted result chunks with optional content extraction. Better than raw search-engine scraping for fact-finding, news, and current-state queries. Pairs naturally with `fetch` (read the top result in full) and `firecrawl` (deep extraction). Requires a Tavily API key.

entries.name: Adt Add
entries.description: Meta-adder for toolkit artifacts. Single entry point to add a skill, pattern, best-practice, hook, agent, or guide with correct frontmatter + sync to 4 publish targets.

entries.name: Adr Write
entries.description: Capture an Architecture Decision Record — what was decided, why, what alternatives were considered, and when to revisit. Writes a numbered ADR file to docs/adr/ in the active repo, with consistent formatting that downstream skills (improve-codebase-architecture, refactor-plan) can read. Use right after deciding something non-obvious so the reasoning isn't lost.

entries.name: Audit Deep
entries.description: Composite skill — full project health check across testing, config, hooks, performance, security, MCP, and plugins. Runs the audit skills in parallel and reconciles into one severity-ranked report with prioritized remediation plan. Use weekly per active project, before major releases, or as part of quarterly tech-debt review.

entries.name: Adt Auto Invoke
entries.description: Meta-skill — defines when to apply other skills automatically without being asked

entries.name: Adt Bilingual Readme Sync
entries.description: Keep EN/PT README.md pairs in parity across a repo (and across twin repos like ai-dev-toolkit + ai-dev-toolkit-pt-br).

entries.name: Adt Build Fix
entries.description: Local build-error triage. Detects common compile errors and suggests fixes

entries.name: Adt Checkpoint
entries.description: Git-level WIP safety net. Stashes uncommitted work, tags, and pushes to remote

entries.name: Adt CI Watch
entries.description: Inspect current CI state, identify the first real blocker, and keep monitoring until the checks are understandable.

entries.name: Adt Compress Assets
entries.description: Losslessly compress PNG and SVG assets in place and generate .webp siblings for every raster image. Use when the user asks to "compress assets", "optimize images", "shrink the assets folder", or "convert to webp". Runs a deterministic pipeline via oxipng + svgo + cwebp + ImageMagick.

entries.name: Config Drift Detect
entries.description: Audit project gates (jest/vitest coverage thresholds, tsconfig strictness, eslint rules, husky/lint-staged, sonar quality gate, branch protection) and flag values mathematically incompatible with reasonable defaults for the project's app type. Surfaces conflicts BEFORE skills like test-cleanup or ship hit them. Use weekly, automatically, or before any major refactor.

entries.name: Adt Context
entries.description: Optimize context window usage — compact stale data, compress via summarization, preserve active state

entries.name: Adt Context Hygiene
entries.description: Keep sessions focused and efficient — prune stale outputs, preserve active state, recommend compaction

entries.name: Adt Cost
entries.description: Track and report token usage and estimated cost per session, agent, and phase

entries.name: Debug Deep
entries.description: Composite skill — full debugging workflow from "this is broken" to root cause and fix. Chains systematic-debugging (root-cause hypotheses) → tracer agent (evidence walk) → sentry (production correlation if applicable) → ci-watch (regression check) → incident-response (if production-impacting). Use when a bug needs deep investigation, not just a quick fix.

entries.name: Design Build
entries.description: Composite skill — design, scaffold, build, and verify a UI in one workflow. Chains ui-ux-pro-max or web-design-guidelines (audit/principles) → shadcn or tailwind-design-system (component scaffold) → impeccable or frontend-design (build) → webapp-testing (verify). Use when the task is "build this page", "design and implement X", or any new UI surface.

entries.name: Adt Dispatch
entries.description: Spawn parallel subtasks, assign to worker agents, collect results

entries.name: Adt Eval
entries.description: Evaluate LLM outputs systematically — benchmarks, automated metrics, human preference, and regression tracking

entries.name: Adt Fallback
entries.description: Graceful degradation when primary approach fails. Try alternates in priority order; record which path succeeded for future routing.

entries.name: Feature From Zero
entries.description: Mega-composite — full greenfield feature development from idea to live. Chains research-and-decide (validate the idea + capture decision) → scope-and-execute (analysis + plan + implement) → design-build (if UI involved) → fix-the-suite (if tests need work) → merge-confidently → ship-it → knowledge-loop. The single command for "build feature X end-to-end."

entries.name: Fix The Suite
entries.description: Composite skill — diagnose, repair, and validate a test suite end-to-end. Chains test-health (diagnose) → config-drift-detect (gate compatibility) → test-cleanup (prune + add integration tests) → mutation-test (validate survivors) → adr-write (capture decisions) → docs-sync. Use when "the test suite is bad" or you've hit the test-cleanup-bails-at-the-gate failure mode.

entries.name: Adt Force Merge Self PR
entries.description: Merge your own PR through a protected branch when self-approval is impossible. Wraps the enforce_admins toggle pattern with verification before/after.

entries.name: Knowledge Loop
entries.description: Composite skill — query, capture, improve, and persist knowledge in one workflow. Chains recall (RAG query) → sync-memories (write durable note) → rag-curate (improve weak retrievals) → handoff (durable snapshot if session-ending). Use when the work involves "what did we decide", "remember this", "save where we are", or any closing checkpoint.

entries.name: Adt Learn
entries.description: Auto-extract reusable patterns from the current session into skills or memory

entries.name: Adt Loop
entries.description: Autonomous development cycle — plan, implement, test, review, fix, commit, and PR without stopping

entries.name: Mcp Care
entries.description: Composite skill — full MCP server lifecycle audit and repair. Chains mcp-audit (usage stats) → mcp-health (live readiness) → mcp-doctor (fix broken servers) → suggest mcp-builder if a missing capability surfaces. Use monthly or when claude mcp list shows failures, after major MCP server updates, or when adding new MCP capabilities.

entries.name: Adt Mcp Doctor
entries.description: Diagnose and repair failing MCP servers. Captures real launch tracebacks, identifies stale tool/config references, prunes the bad entries, and reconnects. Use when `claude mcp list` shows `Failed to connect`, after upgrading an MCP server (Serena, @modelcontextprotocol/server-*, or vendor MCPs), or when an MCP that worked yesterday silently stopped responding.

entries.name: Adt Mcp Health
entries.description: Validate live MCP provider health — separate config issues from auth and connectivity failures

entries.name: Adt Mcp Patterns
entries.description: Build robust, secure MCP servers — tool schemas, stateful interactions, async patterns, error handling, and testing

entries.name: Adt Mcp Readiness
entries.description: Check whether MCP-backed workflows are usable on this machine

entries.name: Adt Memory
entries.description: Persist decisions, preferences, and project state across sessions using structured memory types

entries.name: Merge Confidently
entries.description: Composite skill — take a PR from "I think it's ready" to merged with full gate verification. Chains pr-merge-readiness (verdict) → ci-watch / gh-fix-ci (if blockers) → gh-address-comments (if review needed) → ship (when verdict is MERGE). Use as the one-call answer to "can I merge this?" instead of running 4 skills sequentially.

entries.name: Adt Model Serving
entries.description: Choose and configure inference servers (vLLM, TGI, Ollama) — quantization, batching, scaling, and serving patterns

entries.name: Adt Multi Agent
entries.description: Orchestrate teams of specialized agents — DAG execution, task distribution, state sharing, failure recovery

entries.name: Mutation Test
entries.description: Run mutation testing against a project's test suite to verify the tests actually catch failures when source code is broken. Use after major test changes, before declaring a suite "good", or when coverage % looks healthy but you suspect tests aren't catching real bugs.

entries.name: Adt Next Priority
entries.description: Decide the highest-value safe thing to do right now in the active repo or workspace.

entries.name: Nextjs Patterns
entries.description: Next.js App Router patterns, best practices, and cache components guidance. Use when building or debugging App Router code, implementing Server Components and Actions, optimizing rendering modes, configuring caching strategies, or migrating to Cache Components. Covers file conventions, RSC boundaries, data patterns, metadata, route handlers, caching modes, partial prerendering, cache invalidation, and Next.js 16+ features.

entries.name: Onboard New Repo
entries.description: Composite skill — first-touch workflow for an unfamiliar repo. Chains adt-repo-intake (understand) → audit-deep (health check) → config-drift-detect (gate audit) → init (CLAUDE.md if missing) → fewer-permission-prompts (prune permission noise). Use when entering a new repo for the first time, taking over from another developer, or after a major repo restructure.

entries.name: Optimize
entries.description: Optimize context usage for the session by reducing bloat, improving token efficiency, and focusing on relevant areas. Use when context is growing large, responses are slow, or switching between unrelated tasks. Covers context analysis, compacting, targeted pruning, MCP-first strategies, and focused scoping by area.

entries.name: Adt Orchestrate
entries.description: Coordinate multiple subagents/worktrees for parallel workstreams. Decomposes larger tasks into independent sub-tasks, dispatches each to a dedicated agent.

entries.name: Adt Parallel Investigate
entries.description: Fan out N independent investigations as parallel agents in a single tool-use block, then roll up the results. Use whenever the same question must be answered against multiple repos, PRs, or services.

entries.name: Adt Plan
entries.description: Analyze the codebase and create a structured implementation plan before writing any code

entries.name: Adt Plan Change
entries.description: Plan a code change before editing — identify files, sequence steps, set verification criteria

entries.name: Adt Plugin Audit
entries.description: Diff the Anthropic `claude-plugins-official` marketplace against installed plugins and surface the delta, filtering known-unwanted entries (language LSPs you don't use, SaaS integrations you don't use, sample/demo plugins).

entries.name: Pr Merge Readiness
entries.description: Aggregate every signal that gates a PR merge (CI, reviews, conflicts, branch staleness, security scans, third-party reviewers like CodeRabbit/Greptile/Sonar) into a single readiness verdict — MERGE / WAIT / FIX. Use as the one-call check before clicking merge instead of running ci-watch + gh-fix-ci + manual scan.

entries.name: Production Incident
entries.description: Composite skill — full production incident workflow from alert to post-mortem. Chains sentry (triage incoming events) → debug-deep (root cause) → incident-response (coordinate fix + comms) → ship-it (rollback or hotfix deploy) → adr-write (post-mortem). Use when production is on fire — not for dev-time debugging (use debug-deep alone for that).

entries.name: Adt Rag
entries.description: Build and debug Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipelines — chunking, embedding, retrieval, reranking

entries.name: Adt Rag Context Pack
entries.description: Build a task-aware context bundle (relevant code + applicable standards + related past decisions) via the local RAG index, capped at a token budget. Use at the start of any implementation/refactor/debug task instead of reading files blindly. Replaces "read whole file" with "retrieve the function + callers + rules + prior ADR."

entries.name: Adt Rag Coverage
entries.description: Audit corpus distribution by source type and repo; identify coverage gaps and underindexed topics

entries.name: Adt Rag Curate
entries.description: Manually improve corpus quality by adding missing docs and filling retrieval gaps

entries.name: Adt Rag Drift
entries.description: Detect and fix stale chunks from deleted or modified files

entries.name: Adt Rag Index Rebuild
entries.description: Trigger a full or incremental reindex of the RAG corpus

entries.name: Adt Rag Inspect
entries.description: Examine what's actually stored in the index for specific items

entries.name: Adt Rag Quality
entries.description: Evaluate retrieval quality by analyzing query logs and running test queries

entries.name: Adt Rag Recall
entries.description: Semantic-search personal knowledge (memory, plans, handoffs, skills, Codex rules) via the local RAG index at ~/.claude/rag-index/. Use when a query is fuzzy or cross-file ("how did we fix X", "what did we decide about Y", "which skill handles Z"). Complements grep (exact) and Serena (code symbols). If the user asks a recall question that doesn't map to a specific known file, reach here first.

entries.name: Refactor Pipeline
entries.description: Composite skill — safely refactor a module end-to-end with sequencing, parallel implementation, post-refactor cleanup, and rationale capture. Chains refactor-plan (phased plan + rollback) → three-man-team (architect/builder/reviewer in parallel) → fix-the-suite post-refactor → adr-write → docs-sync. Use for non-trivial refactors that need both careful sequencing and durable record.

entries.name: Adt Release Flow
entries.description: Ship validated changes with repeatable release evidence — version bump, changelog, tag, and optional GitHub release

entries.name: Adt Repo Intake
entries.description: Onboard into an unfamiliar repository fast before making any changes

entries.name: Adt Research
entries.description: Deep research on a topic using web search, docs, and codebase analysis

entries.name: Research And Decide
entries.description: Composite skill — research a question, challenge the conclusion, plan adoption, and capture the decision. Chains adt-research or brainstorming (explore) → critic agent (challenge) → plan (sequence) → adr-write (record). Use when evaluating a library / pattern / architecture choice — forces the research-to-ADR pairing that usually slips.

entries.name: Adt Resume
entries.description: Recover session state from git, plans, and open PRs, then continue from the last completed phase without repeating work

entries.name: Adt Route
entries.description: Route a task to the most fitting skill or agent. Parses user intent, checks installed-skill inventory, and picks the best match (single skill or chain).

entries.name: Adt Schedule
entries.description: Define and manage recurring automated agent runs — CI monitoring, dep updates, security scans

entries.name: Scope And Execute
entries.description: Composite skill — understand a problem area, plan the work, execute it, and ship it in one chained workflow. Chains adt-repo-intake or ecosystem-health (analysis) → context-pack (load context) → plan (phased plan) → dispatch or loop (execute) → ship (merge). Use when the task is "fix the X area" or "implement Y end-to-end" — the single command for analysis through merge.

entries.name: Adt Secure
entries.description: Shortcut for security review on current change set. Runs layered checks (secret-scan, dep-audit, semgrep, OWASP patterns, prompt-injection review).

entries.name: Security Sweep
entries.description: Composite skill — full security pass across secrets, dependencies, code paths, and OWASP risks. Chains security-audit (broad) + socket-audit (npm supply chain) + semgrep (pattern scan) + code-security (code review for vulns) in parallel, reconciles into one severity-ranked report with remediation plan. Use quarterly per active repo or before any release of security-sensitive code.

entries.name: Adt Self Heal
entries.description: Autonomous error recovery — detect failures, diagnose root cause, apply fixes, and resume without stopping

entries.name: Seo A11y Audit
entries.description: Composite skill — full SEO + Accessibility audit of a website or webapp in one workflow. Chains audit-website (squirrelscan SEO + tech + perf scan) + seo-audit (SEO framing) + playwright-best-practices (axe-core a11y) + ui-audit (design-system accessibility patterns) → reconciled severity-ranked report split by SEO vs A11y vs Both. Use for periodic site health checks, pre-launch audits, or when search rankings drop.

entries.name: Session Bootstrap
entries.description: Composite skill — start-of-day routine. Chains wake-up (compact brief) → next-priority (decide) → pr-snapshot (PR queue) → context-pack (load relevant context if work-intent). Replaces "what was I doing", "what's next", "load context" with one invocation. Auto-fires on the first non-trivial prompt of a fresh session.

entries.name: Adt Ship
entries.description: Commit, push, and open a pull request with a conventional commit message

entries.name: Adt Ship Check
entries.description: Verify work before committing, pushing, or requesting review

entries.name: Ship It
entries.description: Composite skill — take a merged PR all the way to production. Chains version-bump → changelog-update → ship (tag + release) → deployment-automation or vercel-deploy / cloudflare-deploy / prod-rebuild → post-deploy verify (sentry + ci-watch). Distinct from merge-confidently which stops at merge; this one ends with "live in prod, verified, no incidents."

entries.name: Smart Commands
entries.description: Decision guide for when to proactively use Claude Code slash commands — /think, /model, /compact, /clear. Use when choosing whether to invoke a command before or during a task, or when a complexity-classifier hook has flagged a task as high or critical. Pairs with the complexity-classifier UserPromptSubmit hook.

entries.name: Smart Model Route
entries.description: Auto-classify prompt complexity via Haiku (with heuristic fallback) and inject model routing hints for Agent tool calls. Wired as a UserPromptSubmit hook — fires on every prompt, routes low tasks to Haiku, standard to Sonnet, complex to Sonnet with thorough reasoning, and critical (security/arch/migrations) to Opus.

entries.name: Adt Specs Aggregate Roadmap
entries.description: Regenerate the cross-repo roadmap aggregate in your Claude memory directory by walking every curated repo's `docs/specs/**` frontmatter. Run at session start for cross-repo orientation or after any `spec-new`/`spec-ship` in any repo.

entries.name: Adt Specs Roadmap Refresh
entries.description: Regenerate `docs/roadmap.md` for a repo from all specs under `docs/specs/` + their frontmatter (status, tags, pr). Produces Now/Next/Recently-shipped sections. Run after creating / shipping a spec, or at the start of a new session for orientation.

entries.name: Adt Specs Spec New
entries.description: Create a committed per-feature spec under `docs/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>/{spec,tasks}.md` with frontmatter (status, created, owner, pr, tags). Promotes an ephemeral `.claude/plans/<name>.md` into a persistent, searchable artifact. Use when starting a feature that's larger than a one-shot PR.

entries.name: Adt Sync Memories
entries.description: Sync durable project or session knowledge into the available memory systems so future sessions have accurate context. Use when meaningful work is complete and the user wants the result remembered.

entries.name: Adt Sync PT Parity
entries.description: Sync files (skills/patterns/agents/specs) from EN canonical repo to PT mirror, inserting "Tradução pendente" blockquote after YAML frontmatter, then open PR.

entries.name: Adt Tdd
entries.description: Test-driven development — write tests first, implement second, refactor third

entries.name: Test Cleanup
entries.description: Audit and prune a bloated test suite down to the minimum tests that hit the coverage threshold and guard real behavior. Replaces many shallow unit tests with fewer well-scoped integration tests. Use when test count is disproportionate to app size.

entries.name: Test Health
entries.description: Read-only diagnostic that reports the state of a project's test suite — count vs. proportionality, coverage vs. threshold, runtime, slowest tests, flaky test estimate, dead tests, and which other testing skills to run next. Use as the entry point before any test cleanup, generation, or mutation work.

entries.name: Adt Ticket
entries.description: Assemble a Jira/Linear/GitHub-Issue body from recent commits, diff, and branch context

entries.name: Token Audit
entries.description: Audit historical Claude Code token usage from JSONL session files — shows total spend, cache hit rates, top costly sessions, weekly trends, and where tokens are going. Run after heavy sessions or weekly to measure optimization impact.

entries.name: Adt Toolkit Sync
entries.description: Bump the ai-dev-toolkit version pin in a downstream consumer repo and verify the sync path

entries.name: Ui Audit
entries.description: UI/UX design system reference, pattern library, and focused review guidance for web and mobile applications. Use for targeted design research, pattern selection, UX audits, accessibility guidance, palette and typography selection, layout heuristics, and component pattern research. Covers 50+ styles, 97 color palettes, 57 font pairings, Web Interface Guidelines compliance, and concrete visual/usability improvements for modern SaaS and product interfaces.

entries.name: Adt Unstick PR
entries.description: Recover a PR whose head SHA disagrees with its branch ref (webhook desync). Force-push and close+reopen rarely fix it — recreate the PR.

entries.name: Vercel Patterns
entries.description: React composition and performance optimization patterns from Vercel. Use when building component libraries, refactoring components with prop proliferation, optimizing React/Next.js performance, or designing scalable component architectures. Covers composition patterns, React 19 APIs, performance optimization across waterfalls, bundle size, server/client rendering, and re-render optimization.

entries.name: Adt Verify
entries.description: Run full quality gate suite before committing or creating a PR

entries.name: Adt Worktree Flow
entries.description: Decide when and how to use git worktrees for isolated parallel work

entries.name: Agent Development
entries.description: Guidance for authoring Claude Code subagents — frontmatter fields, when to set a description, examples, tools, colors, triggering conditions, and best practices for autonomous-agent design.

entries.name: AI Agent Gotchas
entries.description: AI coding agents fail in consistent, predictable ways. They fabricate npm packages that don't exist. They catch errors and continue silently. They add features you never asked for. They lose context mid-session and forget what they were doing.

entries.name: Brainstorming
entries.description: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

entries.name: Branch Hygiene
entries.description: Composite skill — one-pass cleanup of stale local branches, dead worktrees, merged branches, and abandoned remote PR branches. Chains `git fetch --prune` → clean_gone → worktree prune → delete merged-to-main/release branches → delete remote PR branches whose PRs merged >7 days ago. Use instead of running clean_gone alone; that only catches half the rot.

entries.name: Claude Api
entries.description: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements). TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (O

entries.name: Claude Automation Recommender
entries.description: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know which Claude Code features to use.

entries.name: CLAUDE.md Improver
entries.description: Audit and improve CLAUDE.md files in repositories. Scans for all CLAUDE.md files, evaluates quality against templates, outputs a quality report, then makes targeted updates. Use when user asks to check, audit, update, improve, or fix CLAUDE.md — or mentions 'CLAUDE.md maintenance' or 'project memory optimization'.

entries.name: AI-Assisted Code Review
entries.description: Code review is time-consuming and inconsistent. Reviewers miss security issues, overlook edge cases, or focus on style over substance. Meanwhile, AI agents catch these mechanical issues instantly but can't judge business logic correctness, UX decisions, or architectural fit.

entries.name: Codex Bridge
entries.description: Import forgekit skills into the OpenAI Codex agent surface. Maps skill markdown to the AGENTS.md conventions Codex uses, wires context injection, and documents which skill types work with Codex's tool-call model.

entries.name: Prompt Injection Defense — Codex
entries.description: Port of the forgekit prompt-injection-defense skill for the OpenAI Codex tool-use surface. Guards against indirect injection through file contents, web fetch results, and tool outputs by adding structured output validation and a sandboxed tool confirmation step.

entries.name: Command Development
entries.description: Guidance for creating Claude Code slash commands — YAML frontmatter, dynamic arguments, bash execution, file references, interactive AskUserQuestion patterns, and best practices for organizing custom commands.

entries.name: Context Building
entries.description: AI agents are only as good as the context they receive. A powerful model with zero project context produces generic code. A mediocre model with excellent context produces code that fits your project perfectly.

entries.name: Create Subagent
entries.description: Design and author reusable subagents for specialized AI tasks. Use when the task needs an isolated agent persona, scoped system prompt, or reusable domain-specific assistant rather than a general skill.

entries.name: Cursor MCP Bridge
entries.description: Register forgekit MCP servers with Cursor's MCP support. Covers editing ~/.cursor/mcp.json, validating server connectivity via Cursor's MCP panel, and troubleshooting the most common auth and transport failures.

entries.name: Cursor Rules Import
entries.description: Port forgekit skills to ~/.cursor/rules/ format. Converts skill markdown to Cursor's .mdc glob-scoped rule files, maps auto-invoke triggers to alwaysApply and file-pattern matchers, and documents which skill categories need to be split per rule.

entries.name: Cursor Tab Mode
entries.description: Guidance on using Cursor's Tab (inline completion) and Composer (multi-file agent) modes for skill execution. Maps forgekit skill types to the appropriate Cursor mode and explains how to trigger skills without a slash-command surface.

entries.name: Debug
entries.description: Systematic root-cause analysis for bugs, errors, and unexpected behavior. Prompts the agent to gather evidence, form competing hypotheses, and rule them out one at a time before proposing a fix — instead of pattern-matching to the most recent similar issue. Triggers on phrases like 'why is this failing', 'investigate', 'root cause', 'ultradebug'.

entries.name: Dep Sweep
entries.description: Composite skill — batch-process the queue of open Dependabot / Renovate / npm-bot PRs by grouping them by risk, auto-merging the safe ones into `release`, and surfacing only the risky ones for human review. Chains gh PR enumeration → risk classification → pr-merge-readiness per group → auto-merge safe → flag risky. Reduces a 20-PR queue to 'merged 14, 6 need eyes'.

entries.name: Dev Assets Sync
entries.description: On-demand backup sync of local Claude Code and Codex state — configs, memories, standards, hooks, skill indexes — to a private dev-assets repository. Use when the user wants to back up the current workstation, refresh dev-assets before a clean-OS install, or capture a snapshot before a risky change. Workspace-wide backup; not a substitute for normal git commits.

entries.name: Doc Coauthoring
entries.description: Guide users through a structured workflow for co-authoring documentation. Use when user wants to write documentation, proposals, technical specs, decision docs, or similar structured content. This workflow helps users efficiently transfer context, refine content through iteration, and verify the doc works for readers. Trigger when user mentions writing docs, creating proposals, drafting specs, or similar documentation tasks.

entries.name: Docx
entries.description: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments,

entries.name: Eng Api Design Reviewer
entries.description: API Design Reviewer

entries.name: Eng Api Test Suite Builder
entries.description: Use when the user asks to generate API tests, create integration test suites, test REST endpoints, or build contract tests.

entries.name: Eng CI CD Pipeline Builder
entries.description: CI/CD Pipeline Builder

entries.name: Eng Code Tour
entries.description: Use when the user asks to create a CodeTour .tour file — persona-targeted, step-by-step walkthroughs that link to real files and line numbers. Trigger for: create a tour, onboarding tour, architecture tour, PR review tour, explain how X works, vibe check, RCA tour, contributor guide, or any structured code walkthrough request.

entries.name: Eng Database Designer
entries.description: Use when the user asks to design database schemas, plan data migrations, optimize queries, choose between SQL and NoSQL, or model data relationships.

entries.name: Eng Dependency Auditor
entries.description: Dependency Auditor

entries.name: Eng Docker Development
entries.description: Docker and container development agent skill and plugin for Dockerfile optimization, docker-compose orchestration, multi-stage builds, and container security hardening. Use when: user wants to optimize a Dockerfile, create or improve docker-compose configurations, implement multi-stage builds, audit container security, reduce image size, or follow container best practices. Covers build performance, layer caching, secret management, and production-ready container patterns.

entries.name: Eng Env Secrets Manager
entries.description: Env & Secrets Manager

entries.name: Eng Focused Fix
entries.description: Use when the user asks to fix, debug, or make a specific feature/module/area work end-to-end. Triggers: 'make X work', 'fix the Y feature', 'the Z module is broken', 'focus on [area]'. Not for quick single-bug fixes — this is for systematic deep-dive repair across all files and dependencies.

entries.name: Eng Git Worktree Manager
entries.description: Git Worktree Manager

entries.name: Firecrawl
entries.description: Web scraping, search, crawling, and page interaction via the Firecrawl CLI. Covers real-time web search with full page content extraction, single-page scraping, multi-page crawls, site mapping, file downloads, and browser interaction. Use when the user wants to search the web, scrape a URL, extract data from a website, or interact with pages requiring clicks or logins.

entries.name: Firecrawl Agent
entries.description: AI-powered autonomous data extraction that navigates complex sites and returns structured JSON. Use this skill when the user wants structured data from websites, needs to extract pricing tiers, product listings, directory entries, or any data as JSON with a schema. Triggers on "extract structured data", "get all the products", "pull pricing info", "extract as JSON", or when the user provides a JSON schema for website data. More powerful than simple scraping for multi-page structured extraction.

entries.name: Firecrawl Crawl
entries.description: Bulk extract content from an entire website or site section. Use this skill when the user wants to crawl a site, extract all pages from a docs section, bulk-scrape multiple pages following links, or says "crawl", "get all the pages", "extract everything under /docs", "bulk extract", or needs content from many pages on the same site. Handles depth limits, path filtering, and concurrent extraction.

entries.name: Firecrawl Download
entries.description: Download an entire website as local files — markdown, screenshots, or multiple formats per page. Use this skill when the user wants to save a site locally, download documentation for offline use, bulk-save pages as files, or says "download the site", "save as local files", "offline copy", "download all the docs", or "save for reference". Combines site mapping and scraping into organized local directories.

entries.name: Firecrawl Instruct
entries.description: Control a live browser session on any scraped page — click buttons, fill forms, navigate flows, and extract data using natural language or code. Use when content is behind JavaScript interaction: login flows, form submission, pagination, infinite scroll, or multi-step wizards. Also handles authenticated scraping via browser profiles.

entries.name: Firecrawl Map
entries.description: Discover and list all URLs on a website, with optional search filtering. Use this skill when the user wants to find a specific page on a large site, list all URLs, see the site structure, find where something is on a domain, or says "map the site", "find the URL for", "what pages are on", or "list all pages". Essential when the user knows which site but not which exact page.

entries.name: Firecrawl Scrape
entries.description: Extract clean markdown from any URL, including JavaScript-rendered SPAs. Use this skill whenever the user provides a URL and wants its content, says "scrape", "grab", "fetch", "pull", "get the page", "extract from this URL", or "read this webpage". Handles JS-rendered pages, multiple concurrent URLs, and returns LLM-optimized markdown. Use this instead of WebFetch for any webpage content extraction.

entries.name: Firecrawl Search
entries.description: Web search with full page content extraction. Use this skill whenever the user asks to search the web, find articles, research a topic, look something up, find recent news, discover sources, or says "search for", "find me", "look up", "what are people saying about", or "find articles about". Returns real search results with optional full-page markdown — not just snippets. Provides capabilities beyond Claude's built-in WebSearch.

entries.name: First PR
entries.description: Composite skill — land a safe first PR in an unfamiliar repo without rediscovery loops or guessing at conventions. Chains onboard-new-repo (architecture + scripts + CI scan) → context-pack (relevant files + standards + ADRs) → scope-and-execute (narrow the task) → test-driven-development (failing test first) → pr-to-release (open the PR). Use the first time you contribute to a repo.

entries.name: Frontend Design
entries.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.

entries.name: Gemini Context Cache
entries.description: Configure Gemini's context caching API to reduce latency and cost for repeated large-context prompts. Covers cache TTL tuning, cache-hit logging, and when caching pays off versus hurts freshness.

entries.name: Gemini Grounding Config
entries.description: Enable and tune Google Search grounding for Gemini API calls. Covers grounding threshold, attribution rendering, dynamic retrieval configuration, and how to combine grounding with forgekit RAG skills for hybrid retrieval.

entries.name: Git Worktrees for Concurrent AI Agent Work
entries.description: Multiple AI agent sessions working on the same repository cause chaos. Session A is on branch feature/auth. Session B switches to main to check something. Session A's next tool call fails because the branch disappeared. Or worse: Session B force-pushes over Session A's work.

entries.name: Handoff Diet
entries.description: Meta-skill codifying the no-wakeup-polling pattern. Describes the ScheduleWakeup anti-pattern (polling every N minutes burns context), the fix (--auto merge + detached watcher script), and when wakeups still make sense (truly unpredictable external state). Reduces handoff spam by ~80% in CI-watching and PR-cascade workflows.

entries.name: Hook Development
entries.description: Guidance for creating Claude Code plugin hooks — PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, SubagentStop, SessionStart, SessionEnd, UserPromptSubmit, PreCompact, Notification events. Covers ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}, prompt-based hooks API, validation patterns, and blocking dangerous commands.

entries.name: Hook Effectiveness
entries.description: Audit Claude Code hooks for fire frequency, latency, exit codes, and output value. Surfaces hooks that never fire, hooks that fire too often (spam), hooks that exit non-zero silently, and hooks that fire but produce no output Claude actually uses. Run weekly; required after wiring new hooks.

entries.name: Hotfix
entries.description: Composite skill — emergency bypass of the release branch when production is broken and waiting for the next release-cut is not viable. Chains incident scoping → branch from main → minimal fix → pr-merge-readiness → merge → patch-tag → deploy → cherry-pick to release → post-deploy verify (sentry + ci-watch). Use ONLY for production-impacting breakage; routine fixes belong in /pr-to-release.

entries.name: Incident Followup
entries.description: Composite skill — runs the postmortem chain after any production incident (hotfix, rollback, or prod outage). Chains adt-research → adr-write → generate-tests → security-sweep (conditional) → knowledge-loop → handoff. Stops the silent-postmortem failure mode where a hotfix ships and the lessons evaporate. Fires after /hotfix Phase 10 or when user says 'postmortem' / 'what did we learn'.

entries.name: Internal Comms
entries.description: A set of resources to help me write all kinds of internal communications, using the formats that my company likes to use. Claude should use this skill whenever asked to write some sort of internal communications (status reports, leadership updates, 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, incident reports, project updates, etc.).

entries.name: LM Studio Import
entries.description: Bring forgekit skills into LM Studio's model context via its local server API. Covers selecting a model in LM Studio, pointing the forgekit CLI at the local endpoint, and a system-prompt template that injects skill markdown into LM Studio sessions.

entries.name: Mac Optimize
entries.description: Diagnose and fix macOS resource pressure for Claude Code and parallel agent workflows. Use when load average is high, swap is saturated, the CLI feels sluggish, or before spinning up multiple worktrees and subagents. Covers CPU top-talkers, swap pressure, zombie claude processes, Node heap tuning, Spotlight and background-agent pruning, and memory purge. Apple Silicon aware.

entries.name: MCP Audit
entries.description: Read-only diagnostic that scans Claude Code session transcripts to surface which MCP servers and tools you actually use, ranked by call frequency, with zero-use servers flagged for removal. Use before authoring an MCP-removal PR or evaluating new servers. Outputs a markdown report; modifies no settings.

entries.name: Mcp Builder
entries.description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).

entries.name: Ollama Runtime
entries.description: Set up Ollama for local model serving compatible with forgekit skills. Covers model selection (Llama 3, Qwen, Mistral, Phi), the OLLAMA_HOST environment variable, concurrent-request tuning, and a compatibility matrix for which skills work with smaller models.

entries.name: Pdf
entries.description: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.

entries.name: Pptx
entries.description: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slid

entries.name: Prompt Injection Defense: Layered Strategies
entries.description: Prompt injection is not a theoretical attack. By Q1 2026, the benchmark is real: CaMeL achieves 67% injection block rate on AgentDojo. NIST AI RMF frames it as agent hijacking. Defense is not a single gate; it's depth.

entries.name: Prompting Discipline
entries.description: How you ask the model matters more than which model you ask. Two disciplines stop most AI coding failures before they start.

entries.name: Release Cut
entries.description: Composite skill — promote the long-lived `release` branch to `main` and cut a single tagged version that batches every PR accumulated since the last tag. Chains pr-merge-readiness → version-bump → changelog-update → ship → stale-branch cleanup. Use instead of releasing every small fix individually so many small commits become one well-scoped release.

entries.name: Repo Bootstrap
entries.description: Composite skill — configure a fresh or under-configured repo for the release-branch workflow in one pass. Creates the `release` branch on origin, seeds Keep-a-Changelog `CHANGELOG.md`, drops in `.claude/dep-sweep-config.json` + `.claude/branch-hygiene-config.json` + a `release-cadence.md` standard stub, opens a `chore/repo-bootstrap` PR to main. Without this, none of the release-branch composites can fire. Idempotent.

entries.name: Skill Creator
entries.description: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.

entries.name: SKILL.md Adoption: Vendor-Neutral Skill Discovery
entries.description: SKILL.md is an open specification released by Anthropic in 2025 that standardizes how tools discover and auto-invoke procedural knowledge (skills, patterns, guides, rules). Instead of each tool (Claude, Cursor, VSCode, CLI) maintaining isolated skill registries, SKILL.md enables a single skill to be published once and auto-invoked across any tool that speaks the spec.

entries.name: Slack Gif Creator
entries.description: Knowledge and utilities for creating animated GIFs optimized for Slack. Provides constraints, validation tools, and animation concepts. Use when users request animated GIFs for Slack like "make me a GIF of X doing Y for Slack."

entries.name: Smart Model Select — Codex
entries.description: Routing skill for OpenAI Codex that picks between GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, o4-mini, and o3 based on task complexity, latency budget, and cost ceiling. Includes a decision matrix and a shell helper that reads CODEX_MODEL_TIER from the environment.

entries.name: Spec Driven Development
entries.description: AI agents are fast at writing code but have no memory of your intent. You describe a feature in a prompt, the agent builds it, and three sessions later a different agent (or you) changes it in a way that drifts from the original requirement. The spec lives only in your head.

entries.name: Task Orchestration
entries.description: You have 5 projects, each with a session. You open each one, paste "continue with next priorities", wait, switch, repeat. You're acting as a human cron job.

entries.name: AI-Assisted Test Generation
entries.description: Test generation is repetitive but critical. Developers skip tests because writing edge cases is tedious. AI agents can generate tests quickly but often produce brittle, unrealistic tests that provide false confidence. Tests that mock internals, use fake data like "foo@example.com", or test implementation details break on every refactor.

entries.name: Vertex AI Setup
entries.description: Set up Vertex AI to run Gemini models with forgekit skills. Covers project and region configuration, service-account key rotation, quota requests, and a one-command verify that confirms the model endpoint is reachable.

entries.name: vLLM Server
entries.description: Configure a vLLM server for running forgekit skills against open-weight models. Covers engine args for throughput vs latency, an OpenAI-compatible endpoint that the forgekit CLI can point at, and quantization options for GPU-constrained setups.

entries.name: Web Artifacts Builder
entries.description: Suite of tools for creating elaborate, multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts using modern frontend web technologies (React, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui). Use for complex artifacts requiring state management, routing, or shadcn/ui components - not for simple single-file HTML/JSX artifacts.

entries.name: Webapp Testing
entries.description: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.

entries.name: Xlsx
entries.description: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my

entries.name: Capture Training
entries.description: Capture prompt/response training data from Claude Code sessions for evals, fine-tuning prep, and workflow analysis. Best on any machine with Python 3; low CPU and RAM needs.

entries.name: Install Macos
entries.description: Bootstrap Homebrew and the core Forge Kit CLI stack on macOS. Use on a fresh machine or to repair drift; recommended for developer Macs with admin access and network access.

entries.name: Install Ubuntu
entries.description: Bootstrap the core Forge Kit CLI stack on Ubuntu with apt-managed dependencies. Best for fresh dev boxes or CI images with sudo access, network access, and modest system resources.

entries.name: Install Windows
entries.description: Bootstrap the core Forge Kit CLI stack on Windows via PowerShell and winget. Use on workstation setup or repair runs; requires administrator access, package-manager access, and standard developer hardware.

entries.name: Mcp Health
entries.description: Validate configured MCP servers for connectivity, auth, and tool/schema health before you depend on them in daily workflows. Best on any Python 3 environment with network access to your MCP targets.

entries.name: Release
entries.description: Run a semver release flow with version bump, changelog generation, tagging, and publish orchestration. Best for maintainers on a clean git tree with Python 3, git, and release credentials ready.

entries.name: Cross-Repo Review Tools Installer
entries.description: Bash installer that wires automated code review across a repo — drops in stack-appropriate Danger, CodeRabbit, and SonarCloud config plus a Dangerfile template tuned for the detected runtime (Node, Python, Go, or mixed). Runs idempotent — re-running re-applies the latest templates without duplicating CI jobs. Best for trunk-based monorepos and feature branches that want CodeRabbit-grade review on every PR without per-repo manual setup.

entries.name: Setup AI Workflow Macos
entries.description: Provision a full Forge Kit AI-assisted workflow on macOS, including tools, defaults, and daily-use integrations. Best for a primary dev machine with admin access, network access, and enough disk for local tooling.

entries.name: Setup Claude Code
entries.description: Configure Claude Code with Forge Kit defaults for MCP servers, hooks, skills, and global settings. Best for developers standardizing a local AI workflow on a machine with existing Claude Code access.

entries.name: Toggle Mcp
entries.description: Enable or disable individual MCP providers quickly while debugging tool conflicts, cost, or startup issues. Best on any Python 3 environment with local access to your editor or agent config files.

entries.name: Validate Companies
entries.description: Validate company profile data against the Forge Kit schema before publishing, syncing, or importing it elsewhere. Best in CI or content-maintenance workflows on any POSIX shell with low resource requirements.
